title: along with the share code, I also want autosync feature using firebase to syn...
body:
## ✨ Feature Request

along with the share code, I also want autosync feature using firebase to sync across devices. If there are credentials needed, Claude can suggest it in the output for the manual steps.

---

**Category:** `enhancement`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
enhanced_description:
Feature Request: Firebase Autosync for Cross-Device Data Sync

What the user reported:
- The app currently has a "share code" feature for cross-device data sharing
- The user wants an additional automatic sync feature using Firebase Realtime Database or Firestore
- If Firebase credentials are needed, the implementation should output manual setup steps

Component/area affected:
- src/ (main app logic, data persistence layer)
- Currently the app uses IndexedDB (local storage) for all baby tracking data
- New: Firebase sync layer on top of existing storage

Expected behaviour:
- When autosync is enabled, any new log entry (feed, sleep, diaper, etc.) is automatically synced to Firebase
- Multiple devices sharing the same Firebase project/sync key can see each other's data in real time
- Data should merge gracefully (no overwrite conflicts between devices)
- Works offline and syncs when connectivity is restored (Firebase handles this natively)

Current behaviour:
- Data is stored locally in IndexedDB only
- Cross-device sharing requires manual export/import via the share code feature

Edge cases to handle:
- Offline-first: data should still be writable offline and sync when online
- Conflict resolution: if two devices log simultaneously, use timestamp-based merge (last write wins per entry key)
- Auth: Firebase anonymous auth or a user-provided project config (no email/password required)
- Credentials: provide manual setup instructions as code comments or console output (e.g. firebaseConfig object that the user pastes in)
- Graceful degradation: if Firebase is not configured, app works exactly as before

Acceptance criteria:
1. A Firebase sync module is added to src/ that integrates with the existing data layer
2. When Firebase is configured (user provides config), all log entries are synced in real time
3. Sync is opt-in — existing functionality is unchanged if Firebase is not configured
4. Manual setup instructions are included (either in code comments or a displayed setup guide)
5. The share code feature continues to work independently

Testing:
- Unit test: sync module correctly reads/writes to Firebase mock
- Unit test: offline writes queue and flush correctly when online
- Unit test: conflict resolution (last-write-wins by timestamp) works correctly
- Unit test: sync disabled gracefully when no Firebase config present
- Integration: two simulated devices sharing same sync key see same data
failure_reason: Claude CLI exited with code 143. Check /Users/akashkg/saanvi/babybloom/bot/claude-issue.log for details.

title: [Deploy Failed] Build failure on 8ecaf58
body:
**Deployment failed** (auto-created by pipeline)

**Commit:** [`8ecaf58`](https://github.com/akashkguw/babybloom/commit/8ecaf58)
**Time:** 2026-03-29 15:10 PDT
**Actions run:** https://github.com/akashkguw/babybloom/actions/runs/23720262007

## Failure Details

**Job:** test (20) — FAILED
**Failed step:** Lint

## Recent Commits

```
8ecaf58 Auto-deploy: update BabyBloom — closes #155
9b8b809 Infra: fix npm ci failure — commit firebase package-lock.json (closes #155)
f865200 Auto-deploy: update BabyBloom
330d3cb Auto-deploy: update BabyBloom — closes #153
474a251 Implement: Bath/sleep mutual exclusions in quick log (closes #153)
```

## Next Steps

1. Check the [failed Actions run](https://github.com/akashkguw/babybloom/actions/runs/23720262007) for full logs
2. Fix the issue locally and push
3. Pipeline will auto-close this issue on next successful deploy

---
_Auto-created by BabyBloom pipeline on deploy failure._
implementation_notes: Fixed ESLint error in src/features/sync/firebaseSyncUtils.ts: removed unnecessary backslash escape of '/' inside a regex character class (no-useless-escape lint rule). Changed `[.#$[\]/]` to `[.#$[\]/]` — specifically `\/` to `/` inside the character class in isValidSyncKey(). No tests added as this is a single-character lint fix with no behavior change.
enhanced_description:
Deploy failure: GitHub Actions Lint step failed on commit 8ecaf58.

What was reported:
- The automated deploy pipeline detected a lint failure in the GitHub Actions "test" job
- The failing commit (8ecaf58) is an auto-deploy that closes #155 (Firebase autosync feature)
- Previous commit (9b8b809) committed firebase package-lock.json to fix npm ci failure

Component/area affected:
- src/ files modified as part of the Firebase integration (#155)
- ESLint is failing on newly added or modified TypeScript/JavaScript source files

Expected behaviour:
- All source files pass ESLint with zero errors
- GitHub Actions "Lint" step exits with code 0

Current behaviour:
- The Lint step in GitHub Actions test job fails
- Deploy is blocked until lint is fixed

Root cause (likely):
- ESLint errors introduced in the Firebase integration code (issue #155)
- Could be unused variables, missing type annotations, improper async/await usage, or other lint rule violations

Steps to fix:
1. Run eslint locally: npx eslint src/ --ext .ts,.tsx,.js,.jsx
2. Review all ESLint errors reported
3. Fix each error in the affected source files (no lint rule disabling unless absolutely necessary)
4. Verify locally: npm run lint (or equivalent) passes
5. Push fix — pipeline will auto-close this issue on next successful deploy

Edge cases:
- Do not disable ESLint rules with eslint-disable comments unless the rule is genuinely inapplicable
- If Firebase-related types are missing, install @types/firebase or use proper Firebase SDK types
- Ensure no "any" type usage if the project has strict no-explicit-any rule

Acceptance criteria:
1. npx eslint src/ exits with code 0
2. GitHub Actions Lint step passes on the next push
3. Full CI pipeline (lint + unit tests + build + smoke) passes
4. Issue is auto-closed by pipeline on successful deploy

Testing:
- Run lint locally before pushing to confirm zero errors
- Confirm no test regressions introduced by the fix
- Add a note in the commit message referencing this issue (#156)
